# Prague city guide

## Overview

Prague (Praha) is a fairy tale city with Gothic spires, Baroque palaces, and cobblestone streets that look like a movie set. It's one of the most beautiful cities in Europe with surprisingly affordable food and beer. The craft beer scene is booming, the architecture is jaw-dropping, and the Old Town Square at night is pure magic.

**Best time to visit**: Spring (Apr-May) and Fall (Sep-Oct) for mild weather and fewer tourists. December for Christmas markets.
**Currency**: Czech Koruna (CZK). 23 CZK ≈ 1 USD. Euros are not widely accepted.
**Language tip**: Czech is one of the hardest European languages, but locals appreciate any effort. "Dekuji" (thank you) goes far.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Stare Mesto (Old Town) | Historic, tourist | Old Town Square, Astronomical Clock, shopping | Metro A, Staromestska |
| Mala Strana (Lesser Town) | Baroque, romantic | Embassies, gardens, cafes, John Lennon Wall | Tram 12/20/22, Malostranske namesti |
| Hradcany | Grand, castle | Prague Castle, cathedral, panoramic views | Tram 22, Prazsky hrad |
| Vinohrady | Trendy, residential | Wine bars, brunch, local dining, parks | Metro A, Namesti Miru |
| Zizkov | Alternative, nightlife | Dive bars, TV Tower, budget eats | Tram 5/9, Lipanska |
| Karlin | Up-and-coming | Modern restaurants, co-working, riverside walks | Metro B, Krizikova |
| Holesovice | Industrial chic | DOX gallery, street food, craft beer | Metro C, Vltavska |
| Nové Město (New Town) | Central, commercial | Wenceslas Square, National Museum, shopping | Metro A/C, Muzeum |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lokál Dlouha | Czech | Stare Mesto | $$ | Traditional Czech food done right. Tank beer (unpasteurized Pilsner Urquell) served directly from the tank. | Svickova (beef in cream sauce), tank Pilsner |
| Cafe Savoy | Cafe/brunch | Mala Strana | $$ | Grand 19th-century cafe with Neo-Renaissance ceilings. Excellent pastries and weekend brunch. | Eggs Benedict, trdelnik, espresso |
| Nase Maso | Butcher/street food | Stare Mesto | $ | A craft butcher shop where you pick your cut and they grill it in front of you. Best burger in Prague. | Beef tartare on toast, cheeseburger |
| Eska | Modern Czech | Karlin | $$ | Innovative Czech cuisine using fermentation and local ingredients. Industrial-chic space in Karlin. | Fermented beetroot, sourdough bread, pork belly |
| U Fleků | Czech beer hall | Nové Město | $$ | Brewing dark beer since 1499. Touristy but historic. The dark lager (tmave pivo) is brewed on-site. | Tmave pivo (dark beer), pork knuckle |
| Kantyna | Butcher/restaurant | Nové Město | $$ | From the team behind Nase Maso. Dry-aged steaks and Czech comfort food in a butcher-shop setting. | Dry-aged steak, beef tartare, craft beer |
| Sisters | Street food | Stare Mesto | $ | Open-faced sandwiches (chlebicky) modernized with premium toppings. Grab-and-go perfection. | Chlebicky (open sandwiches), seasonal specials |
| Cafe Imperial | Cafe | Nové Město | $$ | Stunning Art Deco interior with ceramic-tiled ceilings. Classic Czech dishes and legendary breakfast. | Imperial breakfast, gulas (goulash), coffee |
| Dharma Burger | Burgers | Holesovice | $ | Smash burgers with Czech craft beer. Small menu, big flavors. One of the best burger spots in Europe. | Double smash burger, sweet potato fries |
| La Degustation Boheme Bourgeoise | Fine dining | Stare Mesto | $$$ | Michelin-starred modern Czech tasting menu. Reinterprets 19th-century Bohemian recipes with precision. | Tasting menu with Czech wine pairing |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Prague Castle (Prazsky hrad) | History | Hradcany | $-$$ | 3-4 hours | The largest ancient castle complex in the world. St. Vitus Cathedral inside is stunning. Go early. |
| Charles Bridge (Karluv most) | Iconic | Central | Free | 30 min-1 hour | The 14th-century bridge with 30 Baroque statues. Sunrise is magical before the vendors set up. |
| Old Town Square and Astronomical Clock | Historic | Stare Mesto | Free | 1 hour | The medieval clock puts on a show every hour. The square is stunning surrounded by Gothic and Baroque buildings. |
| St. Vitus Cathedral | Culture | Hradcany | Included with castle | 1 hour | Gothic cathedral with incredible stained glass, including a Mucha window. Inside Prague Castle. |
| Petrin Hill and Tower | Views | Mala Strana | $-$$ | 2 hours | Take the funicular up, climb the mini Eiffel Tower for 360-degree views. Beautiful gardens and orchards. |
| John Lennon Wall | Culture | Mala Strana | Free | 15 min | Constantly evolving graffiti wall that's been a symbol of peace since the 1980s. Bring a marker. |
| Jewish Quarter (Josefov) | History | Stare Mesto | $$ | 2-3 hours | Six historic synagogues and the Old Jewish Cemetery. Moving and beautifully preserved. |
| DOX Centre for Contemporary Art | Art | Holesovice | $$ | 1-2 hours | Thought-provoking modern art in an industrial space. The rooftop airship sculpture is unique. |
| Vyšehrad Fortress | History/Views | Vyšehrad | Free | 1-2 hours | Ancient fortress above the Vltava with panoramic views. Quieter alternative to Prague Castle. |
| Beer Spa | Experience | Multiple | $$ | 1-2 hours | Soak in a bath of warm beer ingredients while drinking unlimited Czech lager. Uniquely Prague. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Zizkov Bar Crawl | Bars | Zizkov | $ | The neighborhood with the most bars per capita in Prague. Dive bars, music bars, and cheap beer everywhere. |
| Hemingway Bar | Cocktail bar | Stare Mesto | $$-$$$ | One of the best cocktail bars in Europe. Absinthe expertise, prohibition-era vibe. Reservation recommended. |
| Cross Club | Club | Holesovice | $ | An industrial art installation that happens to be a nightclub. Steampunk interior, techno music, three floors. |
| Anonymous Bar | Speakeasy | Stare Mesto | $$ | Guy Fawkes mask-themed hidden bar. Creative cocktails in a mysterious underground setting. |
| Jazz Dock | Jazz club | Smichov | $$ | Riverside jazz club with floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the Vltava. Live music nightly. |

## Transit tips

- **Litacka app**: Buy and store tickets digitally. Single ticket (30 or 90 minutes) or 24-hour pass.
- **Metro**: Three lines (A, B, C) covering the city efficiently. Runs 5 AM to midnight.
- **Trams**: Extensive network, especially useful for Mala Strana and Hradcany. Tram 22 is the scenic route.
- **Walking**: Prague's historic center is very compact. Old Town to Prague Castle is a 20-minute walk across Charles Bridge.
- **Airport**: Vaclav Havel Airport. Bus 119 to Metro A (Nadrazi Veleslavin) is the cheapest option. About 40 minutes total.
- **Pro tip**: Avoid exchange offices on the street (terrible rates). Use ATMs from major banks instead.
- **Beer**: Czech beer is world-class and cheap. A pint in a pub costs $2-3. Try Pilsner Urquell, Staropramen, or craft options.

## Useful Czech phrases

| English | Czech | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Ahoj (informal) / Dobry den | AH-hoy / DOB-ree den |
| Thank you | Dekuji | DYEH-koo-yee |
| Please | Prosim | PRO-seem |
| Cheers! | Na zdravi! | nah ZDRAH-vee |
| One beer please | Jedno pivo prosim | YED-no PEE-vo PRO-seem |
| How much? | Kolik to stoji? | KO-lik toh STOH-yee |
| Delicious! | Vyborne! | VEE-bor-neh |
| The bill please | Ucet prosim | OO-chet PRO-seem |
